items.prompt: HBH Numbers tab in docs/GDD.html hand-encodes HP, cost, defense, worker counts, footprint for every v1 building. The .gd files in source/buildings/ are the actual truth. Today caught 3 rows drifted (Manor, House, Research Lab). Structural fix: add a build-time pre-processor to publish-gdd.ps1 that reads source/buildings/*.gd, extracts the @export and const values, and rewrites the Numbers tab rows. Same pattern applies to the Walls + Towers table and Units + Enemies stat tables.

The cross-project-status artifact hardcodes the previous session VM mount root. Each Cowork session gets a new mount root, so the live-refresh git + queue + audit panels silently fail. Fix: discover the mount root dynamically at refresh time via ls -d /sessions/*/mnt/YesAndEverything 2>/dev/null | head -1, then substring-extract the session segment, then build all per-repo paths from that base. Apply to all five hardcoded path references in the artifact JS.

GDD section 9 Tier 4 Wonders Effect column drifts from shipped code on every row and needs author judgement on which design wins. Cornucopia: GDD says +5.0 Provisions/s + cap halo, code is 4.0 Provisions/s no cap halo. Perpetual Engine: GDD says +50 Power capacity, code is 5.0 Power/s + 60 Coal seed. Pyre: GDD says noise emitter, 5000 HP, emergency wave; code is -6/min Terror credit, 1200 HP. Sanctum: GDD says -20/min Terror, code is +1 HP/s/unit map-wide heal. Decision needed: amend section 9 to match code, or rewrite code to match section 9.

items.prompt: exposure: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt in Scheduler is git-tracked and contains a live discord.com/api/webhooks URL (first committed in cf05134, feat(scheduler): v0.2.0). Mitigate: (1) add `scripts/.discord_webhook.txt` to .gitignore; (2) `git rm --cached scripts/.discord_webhook.txt`; (3) commit the redaction; (4) rotate the webhook on Discord (the leaked URL is permanently in git history and trivially recoverable). History scrub via `git filter-repo --path scripts/.discord_webhook.txt --invert-paths` is a separate manual call after rotation. Compare YaC + YaApothecary, which already use `.discord_webhook.txt.example` + gitignored real file.
items.notes: Surfaced by working-tree-scan-weekly 2026-05-29. Same secret-file pattern as the YaB .finances/ exposure earlier in the cycle.

items.prompt: X:\YesAndEverything\.git\index is corrupt: `git status` reports 'index uses ??m@ extension, which we do not understand / fatal: index file corrupt' and `git fsck` reports 'bad index file sha1 signature'. Repository refs are intact (HEAD reachable, last commit 3ff1b68 docs(brackish): publish GDD v0.33.2). Recovery: from the repo root run `rm .git/index && git reset` to rebuild the index from HEAD + the working tree. If that still fails, `git read-tree HEAD` is the second-line fallback. Block the next publish-gdd / status-write / queue-write until the index is clean - any push attempt from this state will fail.
items.notes: Surfaced by working-tree-scan-weekly 2026-05-29. FUSE index corruption hazard; first occurrence in the working-tree scan rotation.

CHANGELOG.md missing entries for v0.31.x tail through v0.33.4 (commits v0.31.7, v0.31.8, v0.32.2, v0.32.3, v0.33.2 at minimum). Reconcile: walk `git log --oneline v0.31.0..HEAD` and prepend a `## [vX.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD` block per missing version. The GDD changelog footer is the in-doc source; CHANGELOG.md mirrors it. Both should agree.

items.prompt: Scheduler working tree has ~75 modified files + 3 untracked carrying uncommitted edits; several were last touched by HEAD well over 7 days ago: apps/api/src/index.test.ts and apps/web/package.json (HEAD 2026-05-10, 19 days), scripts/discord-notify.ps1 and apps/api/wrangler.toml (HEAD 2026-05-18, 11 days). High likelihood of FUSE-truncation risk the longer it sits per `htbh-fuse-edit-tool-truncation` and `commit-per-patch-on-htbh`. If any of it is WIP not ready to ship, stash explicitly so the working tree isn't carrying it forward session after session.

items.prompt: YaC has one stash entry: `stash@{0}: On main: scraper WIP`. Review and either apply (then ship or re-stash with clearer label), or drop. `git -C X:\YesAndChains stash show -p stash@{0}` to inspect; `git stash apply stash@{0}` or `git stash drop stash@{0}` to act. Stashes live in the reflog and can be reconstructed for 90 days, so the safer move is `apply` and decide in-tree, not blind drop.
